title: Polymer clay cloisonne-style enamel pendant
subtitle: A round pendant with raised black cell divisions filled with solid colour clay, sanded flat to read as enamel
excerpt: This 35 mm pendant uses thin black polymer clay snakes as cell dividers in the manner of cloisonne metalwork, filling each cell with differently coloured clay before curing. Sanding back to 1000 grit and buffing levels the surface and reveals a finish that reads convincingly as vitreous enamel. No kiln required.

glossaryTerms.definition: A metalwork enamel technique in which thin metal wires form cell walls (cloisons) that are filled with powdered glass enamel and fired; here reproduced with polymer clay
An individual cell in cloisonne work, defined by the surrounding wire or clay wall and filled with a single colour

content.text: Traditional
cloisonne
uses metal wire to divide enamel colours. This pendant reproduces the same effect using thin black polymer clay snakes as the
cloison

walls and solid-coloured clay filling each cell. After curing, the surface is sanded flat so the black dividers and colour fills are level, reading as a single smooth panel rather than a relief surface. No kiln is required: polymer clay hardens in a domestic oven at 110 to 130 degrees C.

materials.name: Polymer clay, black (backing and cell dividers)
Polymer clay, 3 to 5 colours for cell fills

tools.name: Wooden rolling pin or acrylic roller

content.text: Preparation

Plan the design on paper before working in clay. A geometric pattern with six to eight cells works well for a 35 mm pendant: a central hexagon surrounded by triangular outer cells is a reliable starting point. Floral designs with a central circle and petal cells also work. Keep the cell dividers to a consistent width of approximately 1 mm: thinner dividers are harder to control and more likely to break during sanding. Warm all clay by kneading each colour separately. Wet-dry abrasive paper in 400, 600, 800, and 1000 grit is needed for finishing.
Construction
Roll the black clay into a slab 3 mm thick and cut a circle 35 mm in diameter using the fettling knife guided around a template or circle cutter. This is the backing sheet that the cell walls sit on.

Roll thin snakes of black clay approximately 1 mm in diameter and at least 100 mm long. Roll them by placing a finger on the clay against the work surface and rolling with gentle pressure away from you. Consistent diameter is important: inconsistent dividers will show after sanding.

Press the snakes onto the black backing circle following your planned design, cutting each length precisely with the fettling knife where it meets another divider or the pendant edge. Press each snake down firmly so it bonds to the backing sheet.
Fill each
cloison

cell with a contrasting colour clay. Press small amounts of coloured clay into each cell, pressing firmly to eliminate air gaps and fill the space level with the top of the black cell dividers. Overfill slightly, approximately 0.5 mm above the divider height: the excess is removed during sanding.
Smooth the filled surface gently with a fingertip or wooden rib to level the colour fills as far as possible before curing. Do not press too hard or the black dividers may distort.
Pierce the pendant hole approximately 3 mm from the top edge using the needle tool.
Place on a ceramic tile and
cure
at 110 to 130 degrees C for 30 minutes. Allow to cool completely.
Curing

After curing the pendant face will appear uneven, with cell fills sitting proud of the black dividers in some areas. This is corrected by sanding. The cured piece must be cool and fully hard before sanding begins.
Finishing

Sand the face with 400 grit wet-dry paper using water as lubricant, working in circular motions. The colour fills and black dividers will all sand back to the same level. Continue through 600, 800, and 1000 grit in sequence. At 1000 grit the surface should be level, smooth, and uniformly matte. Buff with a soft cloth to bring up a high gloss. The result at this stage reads clearly as enamel metalwork. A coat of gloss varnish may be applied for additional protection and shine. Attach a jump ring and chain.
Care

This pendant is not food-safe and is for decorative wear only. Do not place in a microwave or dishwasher. Wipe clean with a soft dry cloth. The sanded surface is scratch-resistant but not scratch-proof: store flat or hanging to prevent contact scratches.
Variations
Matching earrings: cut two 18 mm circles as backing sheets and reproduce the same design in miniature.
Translucent fill cells: use translucent tinted clay for cell fills so the black backing shows through as a darker undertone.
Gold-tone dividers: use a gold metallic clay for the cell snakes rather than black, backed on cream for a more traditional cloisonne palette.
Rectangular brooch: use a 50 mm by 30 mm rectangular backing sheet for a bar-brooch format, with a pin finding attached to the reverse after curing.

rows.problem: Cell divider snakes moving out of position while filling the cells
rows.cause: Dividers not pressed firmly enough onto the backing, or the backing is too warm and soft
rows.fix: Press each divider snake down firmly immediately after placing it. Chill the backing on a cool tile for 10 minutes before filling cells so the dividers hold their position

rows.problem: Colour from one cell bleeding into an adjacent cell during filling
rows.cause: Pressing too hard when filling, deforming the divider wall
rows.fix: Fill cells by placing small pieces of clay and pressing very gently from the centre outward. If bleed occurs, remove the offending clay with the needle tool and replace

rows.problem: Divider snakes sanding away completely during finishing
rows.cause: Snakes were too thin (less than 1 mm) or the backing clay was not the same hardness as the fill clay
rows.fix: Roll snakes at a minimum 1 mm diameter. If dividers are lost in a finished piece, paint them back in with acrylic paint and a fine brush after sanding

rows.problem: Colour fills crumbling away from cells during sanding
rows.cause: Clay not pressed into cells firmly enough, leaving voids beneath the surface
rows.fix: Fill cells more firmly at the construction stage. If a fill crumbles during finishing, apply raw clay to the void, cure again for 15 minutes, then re-sand
